title: Analyzing the interference of sound waves and beats
description: Practice analyzing patterns formed when two sound waves interfere.
topic_path: Analyzing the interference of sound waves and beats

assessment_items.question_content:
Two speakers simultaneously create sounds of slightly different frequencies. The variation in air pressure of the individual sound waves is measured by a microphone over time $t$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following times has the lowest sound volume at the microphone?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
When two periodic waves interfere, their amplitudes add up to create a loud and soft alternation of sound volume known as beats.

When two periodic waves have opposite directions of amplitude, they interfere to produce a softer sound.  Alternatively, the waves add up to produce a louder sound when they have the same directions of amplitude.
Since the waves at time $t_C$ have opposite directions of amplitude, its resulting total sound will have the lowest volume.
The correct choice is $t_C$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Two speakers simultaneously create sounds of slightly different frequencies. The variation in air pressure of the individual sound waves is measured by a microphone over time $t$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following times has the most sound volume at the microphone?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
When two periodic waves interfere, their amplitudes add up to create a loud and soft alternation of sound volume known as beats.

When two periodic waves have opposite directions of amplitude, they interfere to produce a softer sound.  Alternatively, the waves add up to produce a louder sound when they have the same directions of amplitude.
Since the waves at time $t_A$ have the greatest amplitudes in the same direction, its resulting total sound will have the most volume.
The correct choice is $t_A$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Two speakers simultaneously create sounds of slightly different frequencies. The variation in air pressure of the individual sound waves is measured by a microphone over time $t$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following times has the lowest sound volume at the microphone?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
When two periodic waves interfere, their amplitudes add up to create a loud and soft alternation of sound volume known as beats.

When two periodic waves have opposite directions of amplitude, they interfere to produce a softer sound.  Alternatively, the waves add up to produce a louder sound when they have the same directions of amplitude.
Since the waves at time $t_C$ have opposite directions of amplitude with similar magnitudes, its resulting total sound will have the lowest volume.
The correct choice is $t_C$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
When two periodic waves interfere, their amplitudes add up to create a loud and soft alternation of sound volume known as beats.

When two periodic waves have opposite directions of amplitude, they interfere to produce a softer sound.  Alternatively, the waves add up to produce a louder sound when they have the same directions of amplitude.
Since the waves at time $t_A$ have the greatest amplitudes in the same direction, its resulting total sound will have the most volume.
The correct choice is $t_A$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Two speakers simultaneously create sounds of slightly different frequencies. The total sound is measured by a microphone over time $t$. The individual pressure amplitude of each speaker is $p$.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following times has a total sound that is louder than the sound from an individual speaker?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The sounds from both speakers combine to form this interference pattern. 

When the two waves constructively interfere, the total amplitude is larger than  $p$ and the volume is louder than a single speaker.  

When the two waves destructively interfere, the total amplitude is  less than $p$.
At time $t_A$ the magnitude of the pressure is greater than $p$.
The correct answer is $t_A$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Two speakers simultaneously create sounds of slightly different frequencies. The total sound is measured by a microphone over time $t$. The individual pressure amplitude of each speaker is $p$.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following times has a total sound that is quieter than the sound from an individual speaker?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The sounds from both speakers combine to form this interference pattern. 

When the two waves constructively interfere, the total amplitude is larger than  $p$ and the volume is louder than a single speaker.  

When the two waves destructively interfere, the total amplitude is  less than $p$.
At time $t_D$ the magnitude of the pressure is less than $p$.
The correct answer is $t_D$.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:

When the two waves constructively interfere, the total amplitude is larger than  $p$ and the volume is louder than a single speaker.  

When the two waves destructively interfere, the total amplitude is  less than $p$.
At time $t_A$ the magnitude of the pressure is greater than $p$.
The correct answer is $t_A$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Two speakers simultaneously create sounds of slightly different frequencies ($f_A$ and $f_B$). The variation in air pressure of the individual sound waves is measured by a microphone over time $t$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**What adjustment can we make to increase the beat frequency?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Beats occur when waves with two different frequencies interfere. The beat frequency increases as the difference between the frequencies increases.  We can do this by adjusting the individual wave frequencies so the wave crests become further apart.

Since period and frequency are inversely proportional, increasing the frequency reduces the time between adjacent wave crests. Oppositely, decreasing frequency increases the time between wave crests. 
The period of wave $\purpleD A$ ($\purpleD {T_A}$) is smaller than the period of wave $\greenD B$ ($\greenD{T_B}$) as shown below.  

[[☃ image 1]]

Decreasing the frequency of wave $\greenD B$ increases its period, moves its crests further apart from the crests of wave $\purpleD A$, and increases the beat frequency. 
The correct answer is:

Decreasing $f_B$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Two speakers simultaneously create sounds of slightly different frequencies ($f_A$ and $f_B$). The variation in air pressure of the individual sound waves is measured by a microphone over time $t$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**What adjustment can we make to reduce the beat frequency?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Beats occur when waves with two different frequencies interfere. The beat frequency reduces as the difference between the frequencies reduces.  We can do this by adjusting the individual wave frequencies so the wave crests overlap.

Since period and frequency are inversely proportional, increasing the frequency reduces the time between adjacent wave crests. Oppositely, decreasing frequency increases the time between wave crests. 
The period of wave $\purpleD A$ ($\purpleD{T_A}$) is smaller than the period of wave $\greenD B$ ($\greenD{T_B}$) as shown below.  

[[☃ image 1]]

Increasing the frequency of wave $\greenD B$ decreases its period, moves its crests to overlap with the crests of wave $\purpleD A$, and reduces the beat frequency. 
The correct answer is:

Increasing $f_B$ .

The period of wave $\purpleD A$ ($\purpleD{T_A}$) is smaller than the period of wave $\greenD B$ ($\greenD{T_B}$) as shown below.  

[[☃ image 1]]

Decreasing the frequency of wave $\purpleD A$ increases its period, moves its crests to overlap with the crests of wave $\greenD B$, and reduces the beat frequency. 
The correct answer is:
